CREATE TABLE businessService (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), description TEXT);
CREATE TABLE bindingTemplate (id INT PRIMARY KEY, service_id INT, binding_data TEXT, FOREIGN KEY (service_id) REFERENCES businessService(id));
INSERT INTO businessService (id, name, description) VALUES (1, 'Example Service 1', 'This is an example business service.');
INSERT INTO bindingTemplate (id, service_id, binding_data) VALUES (1, 1, 'Binding data for service 1');
UPDATE bindingTemplate SET binding_data = 'Updated binding data for service 1' WHERE id = 1;
SELECT * FROM businessService bs JOIN bindingTemplate bt ON bs.id = bt.service_id WHERE bs.name LIKE '%Example%';
INSERT INTO businessService (id, name, description) VALUES (2, 'Example Service 2', 'Another example business service.');
INSERT INTO bindingTemplate (id, service_id, binding_data) SELECT 2, id, 'Dynamic binding for Example Service 2' FROM businessService WHERE id = 2;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bindingTemplate WHERE binding_data IS NOT NULL;
SELECT bs.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bindingTemplate WHERE bindingTemplate.service_id = bs.id) AS binding_count FROM businessService bs;
DELETE FROM bindingTemplate WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM bindingTemplate WHERE service_id = 2);
UPDATE businessService SET description = 'Updated description for service 2' WHERE id = (SELECT service_id FROM bindingTemplate WHERE id = 1);
DELETE FROM businessService WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT service_id FROM bindingTemplate);
SELECT bs.name, bt.binding_data FROM businessService bs LEFT JOIN bindingTemplate bt ON bs.id = bt.service_id WHERE bt.binding_data IS NULL;
